bead.title: routing: emit filter_reason for excluded same-harness tier (no silent absence)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
When a candidate from a multi-tier subscription harness is excluded (e.g., the catalog has no power metadata for that tier), the routing_decision event drops the row silently rather than emitting it with an explicit filter_reason. This makes downstream diagnosis impossible — "absent" is ambiguous between "never enumerated" and "filtered out".

SCOPE
- Verify the routing engine emits an ineligible Candidate row (with FilterReason set) when a same-harness tier is known to the harness (in AutoRoutingModels / SupportedModels) but missing required catalog metadata.
- If absent today, ensure buildHarnessCandidates emits a row with eligible=false, filter_reason=FilterReasonPowerMissing (or similar) instead of dropping it.
- Negative test: stub a claude HarnessEntry with sonnet-4.6 enumerated but ModelEligibility lookup returning power=0 for sonnet — assert the candidate appears in Decision.Candidates with FilterReason=FilterReasonPowerMissing.

DEPENDS ON
fizeau-f7fa1d87 (per-tier enumeration must work first).

CROSS-REF
Parent: fizeau-80d4bf41
bead.acceptance:
1. With a claude HarnessEntry whose AutoRoutingModels include sonnet-4.6 but whose ModelEligibility lookup returns power=0 for sonnet-4.6, routing.Resolve emits a Candidate row for sonnet-4.6 with Eligible=false and FilterReason=FilterReasonPowerMissing (not silent absence).
2. Test in internal/routing/engine_test.go asserts the row is present with the expected FilterReason.
3. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./internal/routing/... ./... green.
4. No regression.
